01_overview.foreign_policy_doctrine: Proactive Contribution to Peace - Free and Open Indo-Pacific
key_principles: Japan-US Alliance as cornerstone of security
Free and Open Indo-Pacific (FOIP) vision originator
Value-based diplomacy (democracy, rule of law, human rights)
strategic_objectives: Respond to China's assertiveness in East China Sea
Address North Korea nuclear and missile threats
Resolve Northern Territories dispute with Russia
Lead on climate and global health
01_overview.global_positioning: World's 4th largest economy ($4.23 trillion GDP, 2024); 3rd largest defense spender globally ($55 billion, 2024); G7 member; leading ODA donor (#4 globally); US treaty ally; Quad founding member; key Indo-Pacific democracy
01_overview.historical_context: Post-WWII pacifist constitution (Article 9); US occupation-era alliance (1951 Security Treaty); economic miracle; 'checkbook diplomacy' evolution to 'proactive pacifism'; Abe doctrine transformation; Kishida-era defense buildup; 2022 strategic documents marked historic shift

constitutional_framework.article_9: Renounces war as sovereign right; no war potential maintenance (reinterpreted)
constitutional_framework.2022_shift: Counterstrike capability acquisition; 2% GDP defense spending

02_immediate_neighbours.note: Japan is an island nation; neighbors are maritime/nearby

countries.name: South Korea (Republic of Korea)
countries.border_status: No land border; maritime boundary; disputed Takeshima/Dokdo

treaties.name: Treaty on Basic Relations
treaties.significance: Normalized relations; $800 million in grants/loans; 'final settlement' of claims

treaties.name: Camp David Trilateral Framework
treaties.significance: US-Japan-ROK institutionalized cooperation; annual summits; security consultations

treaties.name: Japan-ROK Comfort Women Agreement
treaties.significance: Attempted 'final and irreversible' resolution; ¥1 billion fund

recent_developments.event: Yoon-Ishiba phone call after Ishiba becomes PM
Japan-ROK defense ministers meet at NATO Summit
recent_developments.impact: First NATO Summit both attended as AP4
Historic breakthrough; institutionalized trilateral; Yoon-Kishida reconciliation

recent_developments.event: Yoon visits Tokyo - first Korea president in 12 years
recent_developments.impact: Shuttle diplomacy resumed; forced labor solution announced

people_to_people.tourism: 6.9 million Koreans to Japan (2023); 2.3 million Japanese to Korea (2023)
people_to_people.student_exchange: 15,000 Korean students in Japan; 4,000 Japanese in Korea

countries.name: People's Republic of China
countries.border_status: No land border; disputed Senkaku/Diaoyu Islands (ECS); maritime boundary undefined
countries.maritime_issues: East China Sea delimitation; Senkaku sovereignty; ADIZ overlap
key_issues: East China Sea maritime boundary and resources
Chinese Coast Guard and maritime militia incursions (daily)

senkaku_islands.status: Administered by Japan; claimed by China and Taiwan
senkaku_islands.significance: EEZ, fisheries, potential hydrocarbons, strategic location
senkaku_islands.incidents: Daily Chinese Coast Guard presence since 2012 nationalization
senkaku_islands.japan_position: No territorial dispute exists; inherent territory

treaties.name: Treaty of Peace and Friendship
treaties.significance: Normalized relations; anti-hegemony clause; shelved Senkaku

treaties.name: Joint Communiqué (Normalization)
treaties.significance: Ended WWII state of war; Japan recognized PRC

treaties.name: Maritime Communication Mechanism
treaties.significance: Defense hotline; prevent incidents at sea

recent_developments.impact: First Ishiba-Xi summit; discussed 'mutually beneficial strategic relationship'
Major diplomatic incident; anti-Japanese sentiment concerns

recent_developments.event: Chinese military aircraft enters Japanese airspace (first time)
China bans Japanese seafood over Fukushima water release
Japan implements semiconductor equipment export controls

people_to_people.visa_regime: Visa required for Chinese; relaxed for Japanese

countries.name: North Korea (DPRK)
countries.border_status: No direct border; Sea of Japan maritime
countries.diplomatic_status: No relations; no embassy; protecting power Sweden
key_issues: Nuclear and missile programs (primary security threat)

missile_threats.japan_response: J-Alert system; PAC-3/Aegis defense; counterstrike capability development

bilateral_sanctions: Port entry ban for DPRK vessels

recent_developments.impact: Japan condemns; trilateral response with US-ROK
Japan concerned about regional security implications

countries.name: Russian Federation
countries.maritime_boundary: Disputed in areas around Northern Territories
key_issues: Northern Territories dispute (Etorofu, Kunashiri, Shikotan, Habomai)

northern_territories.russia_position: Legitimate result of WWII; will not return
negotiation_history: 1956 Joint Declaration - 2 islands after peace treaty (reaffirmed by Putin)

pre_2022_engagement.approach: Abe's 'new approach' - economic cooperation for territorial progress

russia_response: Suspended visa-free visits to Northern Territories

energy_dependence.sakhalin_2: Japan stakes (Mitsui 12.5%, Mitsubishi 10%); transferred to Russian entity

recent_developments.event: Russian naval vessels circle Japan (joint with China)

recent_developments.impact: Japan concern over collective defense clause

recent_developments.event: One year Ukraine invasion - Japan continues strong support
Russia suspends Northern Territories visa-free visits

japan_ukraine_support.limitations: No lethal weapons due to export principles (though relaxed)

countries.name: Taiwan
key_issues: Peace and stability in Taiwan Strait (core interest)
China pressure over any upgrade of ties

japan_position.taiwan_strait: 'Peace and stability important for international community'

unofficial_framework.taiwan_institution: Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office

key_cooperation: Semiconductor supply chain (TSMC plant in Kumamoto)

recent_developments.event: Japan congratulates Taiwan President Lai Ching-te
recent_developments.impact: China protests; Japan maintains unofficial stance
PM Kishida attended; supply chain milestone

recent_developments.event: Japan includes Taiwan Strait in defense documents

people_to_people.tourism: Pre-pandemic 4.9M Taiwanese to Japan; 2.1M Japanese to Taiwan
people_to_people.sentiment: Japan consistently most favorably viewed country by Taiwanese (85%+ positive)

USA.status: Most important bilateral relationship; treaty ally since 1960; alliance modernization underway

treaties.significance: Article 5 mutual defense; US bases in Japan; Japan provides facilities

alliance_modernization_2024.command_restructure: Upgrade US Forces Japan to 4-star joint command
alliance_modernization_2024.kishida_visit_april_2024: Most significant alliance upgrade in decades

co_development: GCAP (6th gen fighter with UK, Italy)

extended_deterrence.policy: Three Non-Nuclear Principles (no possession, production, introduction)

Japanese companies 1 million US jobs total

Defense Industrial Cooperation, Acquisition, and Sustainment (DICAS) Forum

recent_developments.event: NATO Washington Summit - Japan as AP4

Most significant upgrade since 1960; command restructure; defense industrial cooperation; AUKUS Pillar 2 consideration

Extended deterrence strengthening; new NDS alignment

IND.name: India
IND.status: Fastest growing major relationship; Quad partner; strategic convergence on China

treaties.significance: Enables troop deployments, exercises, and defense cooperation

GBR.status: Key European partner; Indo-Pacific tilt partner; defense industrial collaboration

gcap.significance: Japan's first multilateral defense co-development outside US

opportunities: Semiconductor supply chain leadership (with TSMC investment)
Healthcare and aging society solutions exports
challenges: Demographic decline (population falling since 2010; 1.2 fertility rate)
North Korea nuclear and missile advancement
Trump 2.0 alliance uncertainty and tariff risks
strategic_shifts: From pacifism to 'proactive contribution to peace'
From arms export ban to 'defense equipment transfer'
From bilateral (US) to multilateral (Quad, trilaterals, GCAP)
From economic statecraft to integrated security
From ODA charity to strategic development cooperation

2022_strategic_documents_significance.national_security_strategy: First comprehensive strategy; China as 'greatest strategic challenge'

important_facts: Quad: USA, Japan, India, Australia (revived 2017; leaders 2021)
Japan Article 9: Renounces war; no war potential (reinterpreted)
Japan defense budget: $55 billion (2024); heading to 2% GDP
Japan-India: Special Strategic and Global Partnership
CPTPP: Japan is depositary; 12 members (UK joined 2023)
RCEP: 15 members; world's largest FTA; Japan first FTA with China
Japan UNSC: Non-permanent 12 times (record); seeks permanent seat
G4: Japan, India, Germany, Brazil (UNSC reform)
Three Non-Nuclear Principles: No possess, produce, introduce nuclear weapons

topics: Japan's Free and Open Indo-Pacific vision and India's convergence
Evolution of Quad from dialogue to strategic partnership
Japan-India Special Strategic Partnership - trajectory and challenges
Japan's defense transformation post-2022 strategic documents
Japan-China relations - economic interdependence vs. security competition
US-Japan alliance modernization and regional implications
Japan's role in Indo-Pacific economic architecture (CPTPP, RCEP, IPEF)
Japan's ODA policy and development cooperation
question_patterns: Examine the evolution of Japan's defense policy from pacifism to 'proactive contribution to peace'. What are the implications for the Indo-Pacific region? (250 words)
Analyze the significance of the Quad in the context of the Free and Open Indo-Pacific. How does it benefit India-Japan relations? (250 words)
Discuss the Japan-India Special Strategic and Global Partnership. What are the key areas of cooperation and challenges? (250 words)
Critically examine Japan's 2022 strategic documents. How do they transform Japan's security posture? (250 words)
Evaluate the Camp David Trilateral Framework (US-Japan-Korea). What are its implications for regional security? (250 words)
model_answer_points: Link FOIP to rules-based order and maritime security
Connect Quad evolution to China's assertiveness
Examine Japan's balancing act between China economic ties and US security alliance

fact_based_points: Japan has 4 main islands: Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, Shikoku
Japan-India bullet train project location: Mumbai-Ahmedabad
Sakura: Japan's cherry blossom; national flower

state_psc.relevance: High - Japan investment in many states; infrastructure projects

must_know_facts: Japan defense budget: $55 billion (2024); 2% GDP target by 2027
FOIP: Free and Open Indo-Pacific (Japan concept)
RCEP: 15 members; Japan's first FTA with China
2022 strategic documents: NSS, NDS, DBP - historic defense shift
Japan G4: With India, Germany, Brazil for UNSC reform
Japan nuclear policy: Three Non-Nuclear Principles
Japan UNSC: Seeks permanent seat; elected 12 times (record)
